params.name: definitions

params.name: buildNumber
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that match this build number. Append * to do a prefix search.

params.name: minTime
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that finished/started/queued after this date based on the queryOrder specified.

params.name: maxTime
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that finished/started/queued before this date based on the queryOrder specified.

params.name: requestedFor
params.description: If specified, filters to builds requested for the specified user.

params.name: reasonFilter
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that match this reason.

params.name: statusFilter
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that match this status.

params.name: resultFilter
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that match this result.

params.name: tagFilters
params.description: A comma-delimited list of tags. If specified, filters to builds that have the specified tags.

params.name: properties
params.description: A comma-delimited list of properties to retrieve.

params.name: maxBuildsPerDefinition

params.name: deletedFilter

params.name: queryOrder

params.name: branchName
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that built branches that built this branch.

params.name: repositoryId
params.description: If specified, filters to builds that built from this repository.

tools.name: build-apis_general-settings-get
tools.description: Gets pipeline general settings. Required: organization, project, api-version. Returns the PipelineGeneralSettings.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: api-version
params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.

tools.name: build-apis_general-settings-update
tools.description: Updates pipeline general settings. Required: organization, project, api-version. Optional: auditEnforceSettableVar, buildsEnabledForForks, disableClassicBuildPipelineCreation (plus 16 more). Returns the updated PipelineGeneralSettings.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: api-version
params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.

params.name: auditEnforceSettableVar
params.description: If enabled, audit logs will be generated whenever someone queues a pipeline run and defines variables that are not marked as 'Settable at queue time'.

params.name: buildsEnabledForForks
params.description: Enable forked repositories to build pull requests.

params.name: disableClassicBuildPipelineCreation
params.description: Disable classic build pipelines creation.

params.name: disableClassicPipelineCreation
params.description: Disable classic pipelines creation.

params.name: disableClassicReleasePipelineCreation
params.description: Disable classic release pipelines creation.

params.name: enableShellTasksArgsSanitizing
params.description: Enable shell tasks args sanitizing.

params.name: enableShellTasksArgsSanitizingAudit
params.description: Enable shell tasks args sanitizing preview.

params.name: enforceJobAuthScope
params.description: If enabled, scope of access for all non-release pipelines reduces to the current project.

params.name: enforceJobAuthScopeForForks
params.description: Enforce job auth scope for builds of forked repositories.

params.name: enforceJobAuthScopeForReleases
params.description: If enabled, scope of access for all release pipelines reduces to the current project.

params.name: enforceNoAccessToSecretsFromForks
params.description: Enforce no access to secrets for builds of forked repositories.

params.name: enforceReferencedRepoScopedToken
params.description: Restricts the scope of access for all pipelines to only repositories explicitly referenced by the pipeline.

params.name: enforceSettableVar
params.description: If enabled, only those variables that are explicitly marked as 'Settable at queue time' can be set at queue time.

params.name: forkProtectionEnabled
params.description: Enable settings that enforce certain levels of protection for building pull requests from forks globally.

params.name: isCommentRequiredForPullRequest
params.description: Make comments required to have builds in all pull requests.

params.name: publishPipelineMetadata
params.description: Allows pipelines to record metadata.

params.name: requireCommentsForNonTeamMemberAndNonContributors
params.description: Make comments required to have builds in pull requests from non-team members and non-contributors.

params.name: requireCommentsForNonTeamMembersOnly
params.description: Make comments required to have builds in pull requests from non-team members.

params.name: statusBadgesArePrivate
params.description: Anonymous users can access the status badge API for all pipelines unless this option is enabled.

tools.name: build-apis_latest-get
tools.description: Gets the latest build for a definition, optionally scoped to a specific branch. Required: organization, project, definition, api-version. Optional: branchName. Returns the Build.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: definition
params.description: definition name with optional leading folder path, or the definition id

params.name: branchName
params.description: optional parameter that indicates the specific branch to use. If not specified, the default branch is used.

params.name: api-version
params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1-preview.1' to use this version of the api.

tools.name: build-apis_leases-update
tools.description: Updates the duration or pipeline protection status of a retention lease. Required: organization, project, leaseId, api-version. Optional: daysValid, protectPipeline. Returns the updated RetentionLease.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: leaseId
params.description: The ID of the lease to update.

params.name: api-version
params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.

params.name: daysValid
params.description: The number of days to consider the lease valid. A retention lease valid for more than 100 years (36500 days) will display as retaining the build 'forever'.

params.name: protectPipeline
params.description: If set, this lease will also prevent the pipeline from being deleted while the lease is still valid.

tools.name: git-apis_annotated-tags-create
tools.description: Create an annotated tag. Repositories have both a name and an identifier. Identifiers are globally unique, but several projects may contain a repository of the same name. You don't need to include the project if you specify a repository by ID. However, if you specify a repository by name, you must also specify the project (by name or ID). Required: organization, project, repositoryId, api-version. Optional: message, name, objectId (plus 3 more). Returns the new GitAnnotatedTag.

params.description: If provided, only include history entries created after this date (string)

params.description: Version type (branch, tag, or commit). Determines how Id is interpreted

params.name: showOldestCommitsFirst

params.name: toCommitId
params.description: If provided, an upper bound for filtering commits alphabetically

params.name: toDate
params.description: If provided, only include history entries created before this date (string)

params.name: user
params.description: Alias or display name of the committer

tools.name: git-apis_policy-configurations-get
tools.description: Retrieve a list of policy configurations by a given set of scope/filtering criteria. Below is a short description of how all of the query parameters interact with each other: - repositoryId set, refName set: returns all policy configurations that *apply* to a particular branch in a repository - repositoryId set, refName unset: returns all policy configurations that *apply* to a particular repository - repositoryId unset, refName unset: returns all policy configurations that are *defined* at the project level - repositoryId unset, refName set: returns all project-level branch policies, plus the project level configurations For all of the examples above, when policyType is set, it'll restrict results to the given policy type. Required: organization, project, api-version. Optional: repositoryId, refName, policyType (plus 1 more).

params.name: continuationToken
params.description: Pass a policy configuration ID to fetch the next page of results, up to top number of results, for this endpoint.

tools.description: Create or update pull request external properties. The patch operation can be `add`, `replace` or `remove`. For `add` operation, the path can be empty. If the path is empty, the value must be a list of key value pairs. For `replace` operation, the path cannot be empty. If the path does not exist, the property will be added to the collection. tools.name: git-apis_pull-request-query-get
tools.description: This API is used to find what pull requests are related to a given commit. It can be used to either find the pull request that created a particular merge commit or it can be used to find all pull requests that have ever merged a particular commit. The input is a list of queries which each contain a list of commits. For each commit that you search against, you will get back a dictionary of commit -> pull requests. Required: organization, repositoryId, project, api-version. Optional: queries, results. Returns the new GitPullRequestQuery.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: repositoryId

params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.

params.name: queries
params.description: The queries to perform.

params.name: results
params.description: The results of the queries. This matches the QueryInputs list so Results[n] are the results of QueryInputs[n]. Each entry in the list is a dictionary of commit->pull requests.

If MergeStrategy is not set to any value, the pull request commits will be squashed if SquashMerge == true. The SquashMerge property is deprecated. tools.name: git-apis_pull-requests-get-pull-request
tools.description: Retrieve a pull request. Required: organization, repositoryId, pullRequestId, project, api-version. Optional: maxCommentLength, includeCommits, includeWorkItemRefs. Returns the GitPullRequest.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: repositoryId
params.description: The repository ID of the pull request's target branch.

params.name: pullRequestId
params.description: The ID of the pull request to retrieve.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: maxCommentLength
params.description: Not used.

params.name: includeCommits
params.description: If true, the pull request will be returned with the associated commits.

params.name: includeWorkItemRefs
params.description: If true, the pull request will be returned with the associated work item references.

params.name: api-version
params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.

tools.name: git-apis_pull-requests-get-pull-requests
tools.description: Retrieve all pull requests matching a specified criteria. Please note that description field will be truncated up to 400 symbols in the result. Required: organization, repositoryId, project, api-version. Optional: searchCriteria.creatorId, searchCriteria.includeLinks, searchCriteria.maxTime (plus 9 more). Returns array of GitPullRequest.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: repositoryId
params.description: The repository ID of the pull request's target branch.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: searchCriteria.creatorId
params.description: If set, search for pull requests that were created by this identity.

params.name: searchCriteria.includeLinks
params.description: Whether to include the _links field on the shallow references

params.name: searchCriteria.maxTime
params.description: If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed before this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.

params.name: searchCriteria.minTime
params.description: If specified, filters pull requests that created/closed after this date based on the queryTimeRangeType specified.

params.name: searchCriteria.queryTimeRangeType
params.description: The type of time range which should be used for minTime and maxTime. Defaults to Created if unset.

params.name: searchCriteria.repositoryId
params.description: If set, search for pull requests whose target branch is in this repository.

params.name: searchCriteria.reviewerId
params.description: If set, search for pull requests that have this identity as a reviewer.

params.name: searchCriteria.sourceRefName
params.description: If set, search for pull requests from this branch.

params.name: searchCriteria.sourceRepositoryId
params.description: If set, search for pull requests whose source branch is in this repository.

params.name: searchCriteria.status
params.description: If set, search for pull requests that are in this state. Defaults to Active if unset.

params.name: searchCriteria.targetRefName
params.description: If set, search for pull requests into this branch.

params.name: maxCommentLength
params.description: Not used.

params.name: api-version
params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.

tools.name: git-apis_pull-requests-update
tools.description: Update a pull request These are the properties that can be updated with the API: - Status - Title - Description (up to 4000 characters) - CompletionOptions - MergeOptions - AutoCompleteSetBy.Id - TargetRefName (when the PR retargeting feature is enabled) Attempting to update other properties outside of this list will either cause the server to throw an `InvalidArgumentValueException`, or to silently ignore the update. Required: organization, repositoryId, pullRequestId, project, api-version. Optional: _links, artifactId, autoCompleteSetBy (plus 32 more). Returns the updated GitPullRequest.

params.description: Specify the strategy used to merge the pull request during completion. If MergeStrategy is not set to any value, a no-FF merge will be created if SquashMerge == false. If MergeStrategy is not set to any value, the pull request commits will be squashed if SquashMerge == true. The SquashMerge property is deprecated. It is recommended that you explicitly set MergeStrategy in all cases. If an explicit value is provided for MergeStrategy, the SquashMerge property will be ignored.

params.name: completion-options-squash-merge
params.description: SquashMerge is deprecated. You should explicitly set the value of MergeStrategy. If MergeStrategy is set to any value, the SquashMerge value will be ignored. If MergeStrategy is not set, the merge strategy will be no-fast-forward if this flag is false, or squash if true.

params.name: completion-options-transition-work-items
params.description: If true, we will attempt to transition any work items linked to the pull request into the next logical state (i.e. Active -> Resolved)

params.name: completion-options-triggered-by-auto-complete
params.description: If true, the current completion attempt was triggered via auto-complete. Used internally.

tools.name: git-apis_pushes-get

params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: repositoryId
params.description: The name or ID of the repository.

params.name: pushId
params.description: ID of the push.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: includeCommits
params.description: The number of commits to include in the result.

params.name: includeRefUpdates
params.description: If true, include the list of refs that were updated by the push.

params.name: api-version
params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.

tools.name: git-apis_refs-list
tools.description: Queries the provided repository for its refs and returns them. Required: organization, repositoryId, project, api-version. Optional: filter, includeLinks, includeStatuses (plus 5 more).

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: repositoryId
params.description: The name or ID of the repository.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: filter
params.description: [optional] A filter to apply to the refs (starts with).

params.name: includeLinks
params.description: [optional] Specifies if referenceLinks should be included in the result. default is false.

params.name: includeStatuses
params.description: [optional] Includes up to the first 1000 commit statuses for each ref. The default value is false.

params.name: includeMyBranches
params.description: [optional] Includes only branches that the user owns, the branches the user favorites, and the default branch. The default value is false. Cannot be combined with the filter parameter.

params.name: latestStatusesOnly
params.description: [optional] True to include only the tip commit status for each ref. This option requires `includeStatuses` to be true. The default value is false.

params.name: peelTags
params.description: [optional] Annotated tags will populate the PeeledObjectId property. default is false.

params.name: filterContains
params.description: [optional] A filter to apply to the refs (contains).

params.name: continuationToken
params.description: The continuation token used for pagination.

params.name: api-version

tools.name: release-apis_definitions-list
tools.description: Get a list of release definitions. Required: organization, project, api-version. Optional: searchText, artifactType, artifactSourceId (plus 9 more). Returns array of ReleaseDefinition.

params.name: organization
params.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

params.name: project
params.description: Project ID or project name

params.name: searchText
params.description: Get release definitions with names containing searchText.

params.name: artifactType
params.description: Release definitions with given artifactType will be returned. Values can be Build, Jenkins, GitHub, Nuget, Team Build (external), ExternalTFSBuild, Git, TFVC, ExternalTfsXamlBuild.

params.name: artifactSourceId
params.description: Release definitions with given artifactSourceId will be returned. e.g. For build it would be {projectGuid}:{BuildDefinitionId}, for Jenkins it would be {JenkinsConnectionId}:{JenkinsDefinitionId}, for TfsOnPrem it would be {TfsOnPremConnectionId}:{ProjectName}:{TfsOnPremDefinitionId}. For third-party artifacts e.g. TeamCity, BitBucket you may refer 'uniqueSourceIdentifier' inside vss-extension.json at https://github.com/Microsoft/vsts-rm-extensions/blob/master/Extensions.

params.name: continuationToken
params.description: Gets the release definitions after the continuation token provided.

params.name: queryOrder
params.description: Gets the results in the defined order. Default is 'IdAscending'.

params.name: path
params.description: Gets the release definitions under the specified path.

params.name: isExactNameMatch
params.description: 'true'to gets the release definitions with exact match as specified in searchText. Default is 'false'.

params.name: tagFilter

params.description: Unique identifier of the artifact used. e.g. For build it would be {projectGuid}:{BuildDefinitionId}, for Jenkins it would be {JenkinsConnectionId}:{JenkinsDefinitionId}, for TfsOnPrem it would be {TfsOnPremConnectionId}:{ProjectName}:{TfsOnPremDefinitionId}. For third-party artifacts e.g. TeamCity, BitBucket you may refer 'uniqueSourceIdentifier' inside vss-extension.json https://github.com/Microsoft/vsts-rm-extensions/blob/master/Extensions.

params.description: Releases with given artifactVersionId will be returned. E.g. in case of Build artifactType, it is buildId.

params.description: Gets the soft deleted releases, if true.

params.name: tagFilter
params.description: A comma-delimited list of tags. Only releases with these tags will be returned.

params.description: A comma-delimited list of extended properties to be retrieved. If set, the returned Releases will contain values for the specified property Ids (if they exist). params.description: Releases under this folder path will be returned

params.description: Version of the API to use. This should be set to '7.1' to use this version of the api.
